text:
(a) In this section, “public entity” means a municipality, county, regional mobility authority, or regional tollway authority.

(b) A public entity may contract with a private entity to act as the public entity’s agent in:

(1) the design, financing, maintenance, operation, or construction, including oversight and inspection, of a toll or nontoll facility under Section 222.104 (Pass-through Tolls) (b); or

(2) the maintenance of a state highway or a portion of a state highway subject to an agreement under Section 222.104 (Pass-through Tolls) (d)(1).

(c) A public entity shall:

(1) select a private entity under Subsection (b) on the basis of the private entity’s qualifications and experience; and

(2) enter into a project development agreement with the private entity.

(d) A private entity selected shall comply with Chapter 1001 (Texas Board of Professional Engineers and Land Surveyors) , Occupations Code, and all laws related to procuring engineering services and construction bidding that are applicable to the public entity that selected the private entity.

(e) A public entity may assign the public entity’s right to payment of pass-through tolls under Section 222.104 (Pass-through Tolls) (b) or (d)(1) to the private entity.

(b) A public entity may contract with a private entity to act as the public entity’s agent in:

(1) the design, financing, maintenance, operation, or construction, including oversight and inspection, of a toll or nontoll facility under Section 222.104 (Pass-through Tolls) (b); or

(2) the maintenance of a state highway or a portion of a state highway converted to a toll facility under Section 222.104 (Pass-through Tolls) (c).

(c) A public entity shall:

(1) select a private entity under Subsection (b) on the basis of the private entity’s qualifications and experience; and

(2) enter into a project development agreement with the private entity.

(d) A private entity selected shall comply with Chapter 1001 (Texas Board of Professional Engineers and Land Surveyors) , Occupations Code, and all laws related to procuring engineering services and construction bidding that are applicable to the public entity that selected the private entity.

(e) A public entity may assign the public entity’s right to payment of pass-through tolls under Section 222.104 (Pass-through Tolls) (b) or (c) to the private entity.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 222. Funding & Fed. Aid
##### § 222.1045 — Contracts of Certain Public Entities

(a) In this section, “public entity” means a municipality, county, regional mobility authority, or regional tollway authority.

(b) A public entity may contract with a private entity to act as the public entity’s agent in:

    (1) the design, financing, maintenance, operation, or construction, including oversight and inspection, of a toll or nontoll facility under Section 222.104 (Pass-through Tolls) (b); or

    (2) the maintenance of a state highway or a portion of a state highway subject to an agreement under Section 222.104 (Pass-through Tolls) (d)(1).

(c) A public entity shall:

    (1) select a private entity under Subsection (b) on the basis of the private entity’s qualifications and experience; and

    (2) enter into a project development agreement with the private entity.

(d) A private entity selected shall comply with Chapter 1001 (Texas Board of Professional Engineers and Land Surveyors) , Occupations Code, and all laws related to procuring engineering services and construction bidding that are applicable to the public entity that selected the private entity.

(e) A public entity may assign the public entity’s right to payment of pass-through tolls under Section 222.104 (Pass-through Tolls) (b) or (d)(1) to the private entity.

(b) A public entity may contract with a private entity to act as the public entity’s agent in:

    (1) the design, financing, maintenance, operation, or construction, including oversight and inspection, of a toll or nontoll facility under Section 222.104 (Pass-through Tolls) (b); or

    (2) the maintenance of a state highway or a portion of a state highway converted to a toll facility under Section 222.104 (Pass-through Tolls) (c).

(c) A public entity shall:

    (1) select a private entity under Subsection (b) on the basis of the private entity’s qualifications and experience; and

    (2) enter into a project development agreement with the private entity.

(d) A private entity selected shall comply with Chapter 1001 (Texas Board of Professional Engineers and Land Surveyors) , Occupations Code, and all laws related to procuring engineering services and construction bidding that are applicable to the public entity that selected the private entity.

(e) A public entity may assign the public entity’s right to payment of pass-through tolls under Section 222.104 (Pass-through Tolls) (b) or (c) to the private entity.

*Added by Acts 2005, 79th Leg., Ch. 994 (H.B.2139), Sec. 2, eff. June 18, 2005.*
